entries.author: Ethan
entries.body: LITA is trying to bring robotics to hotels to improve operational efficiency — currently evaluating a Miami hotel deployment. Watch this space. Also: T800 being used in a robot fight out of NYC (Eugene's crew).

entries.author: Raymond
entries.body: Exploring ideas around micron-scale robot memory. Details incoming.

entries.author: Ethan
entries.body: Two signals: (1) Hard for non-Chinese companies to do large manufacturing contracts with Chinese factories — better to be structured as a Chinese entity if you want to own that supply chain. Some companies run American subs as the public face while keeping China-side ops Chinese-owned. (2) Thinking about cyber security for robots and FedRAMP — robotics cos don't want to build compliance layers themselves, which creates a wrapper/incubation opportunity for whoever does. That wrapper owns US market access for foreign hardware.

entries.author: Erik
entries.body: Cyber layer + US market safety is an incubation opportunity. Robotics companies don't want to spend on compliance or certification — whoever builds the wrapper controls access to the US market for foreign hardware.

entries.author: Erik
entries.body: Two observations: (1) Hard for non-Chinese companies to do large manufacturing contracts with Chinese factories — better to be structured as a Chinese entity if you want to own that supply chain. Some companies are solving this with American subsidiaries as the public face while keeping China-side ops Chinese-owned. (2) Robotics companies don't want to spend on cyber compliance or US safety certification — this is an incubation opportunity. Whoever builds the cyber/compliance wrapper layer owns access to the US market for foreign robot hardware.
